records.summary: An anteroposterior (AP) chest X-ray of a female patient, dated March 20, 2026, showing bilateral lung fields with diffuse opacities and a visible cardiac silhouette.

records.stage_evidence: Diagnostic report with diagnosis and impression

ocr.verbatim:
NAME : 
REF BY.: DR.
65 YRS /FEMALE
DATE: 20/03/2026

X-RAY CHEST PA

DIAGNOSIS
• Mild cardiomegaly is seen.
• Hila are hazy.
• Both lungs are shown fibrosis s/o copd.
• Both CP angles are obtuse.
• Both domes of diaphragm are normal.
• Soft tissue and rib cage are normal.

IMPRESSION
• MILD CARDIOMEGALY.
• BRONCHOVESUCULAR MARGIN PROMINANCE S/O COPD?

• Radiological diagnosis is solely interpretation of images produced by tissues and may not be always conclusive.
• Please intimate regarding typing errors for correction within 7 days.
• Further clinical correlation for final diagnosis. If required feel free to discuss.

MB [Radio-Diagnosis)
Con [Radiologist]
REGN.UPMC

records.summary: This is a typed radiology report for a PA chest X-ray of a 65-year-old female patient. The report details findings including mild cardiomegaly, hazy hila, fibrosis in both lungs suggestive of COPD, obtuse costophrenic angles, and normal diaphragm domes. The impression suggests mild cardiomegaly and bronchovascular margin prominence possibly due to COPD.
key_observations: Diagnosis includes mild cardiomegaly and hazy hila.
Findings suggest fibrosis in both lungs consistent with COPD.
Report includes disclaimer about radiological interpretation and need for clinical correlation.
Report is signed by a radiologist with registration number UPMC.
